Summary Matrix.Tolaram Group \u2014 AI Use Case Priorit: Each use case scored 0–100 on three axes: Business Impact · Implementation Effort (higher = harder) · Data Readiness   |   Click rows to see detail in 'Scoring Detail' sheet
NOTE: Effort scores are inverted — higher score means MORE difficult. Best candidates: High Impact + High Data Readiness + Low/Medium Effort.

Scoring Detail.Tolaram Group \u2014 AI Use Case Scoring: Full scoring rationale for each use case across all three axes. Scores reflect Tolaram-specific context: SAP S/4HANA rollout status, Nigeria infrastructure, multi-JV complexity.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5:
Business Impact — Rationale
(Revenue · Cost · Risk value delivered)
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9:
Implementation Effort — Rationale
(Team · Timeline · Algorithmic complexity · Infrastructure)
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13:
Data Readiness — Rationale
(SAP availability · Data quality · Gaps & mitigations)

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Tolaram ships ~500M noodle packets/year. A 5% forecast accuracy gain = multi-billion Naira inventory savings. Reduces stock-outs (revenue loss) and overstock (working capital waste) across all JV partners.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Revenue recovery from fewer stock-outs; working capital release; better production scheduling at 25 Nigeria factories.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Standard algorithms (ARIMA, Prophet, XGBoost) — low technical risk. 8–12 weeks to pilot top 50 Multipro SKUs. Main effort: SAP data extraction and feature engineering, not model complexity.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: 1 DS + 1 DE. Timeline: 8–12 wks. Algorithm risk: Low. Integration: SAP ERP via standard API.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: SAP S/4HANA sales history already migrated (Ghana, partial Nigeria). Promotional calendars accessible. Only gap: real-time POS from small distributors — not required for v1.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP sales history: High. Promo calendars: Medium. Holiday data: High. POS data: Low (not needed for v1).

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: 1,000+ Multipro distributors — losing one leaves a territory uncovered for weeks. Proactive retention estimated at 5% sales uplift. Medium because it prevents loss rather than drives net new growth.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Distributor retention value; targeted promo uplift on retained accounts; reduced recruitment cost.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: K-Means + XGBoost churn — standard, well-documented. Salesforce CRM already deployed at Multipro, reducing integration effort. Main effort: RFM feature design from order data.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: 1 DA + CRM manager. Timeline: 6–8 wks. Integration: Salesforce CRM already live.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: ERP order history, payment records, CRM data all available for established distributors. Smaller distributors have thinner data — mitigated by aggregate features. No external data required.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP order history: High. Payment records: High. Salesforce CRM: Medium. Small distributor data: Low (aggregate workaround).

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: FMCG companies waste 15–25% of revenue on ineffective promotions. Even 5–10% margin lift on trade spend across Indomie, Kellogg's, Colgate, Dano = hundreds of millions of Naira. Price elasticity modelling critical during NGN inflation cycles.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Trade promo ROI improvement; margin lift on pricing decisions; reduced spend on zero-lift promotions.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Causal inference models (DID, Synthetic Control) are more complex than standard ML. Requires clean promo history + A/B test infrastructure. 4–6 months. Needs senior DS with causal modelling experience.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: Senior DS + PM + Retail Ops. Timeline: 4–6 months. Algorithm risk: High (causal methods). A/B test infrastructure needed.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Promo history in SAP but quality is Medium — promotions not always cleanly coded with mechanic, start/end, sell-out outcomes. Competitor pricing data is Low. Requires 12+ months clean history before modelling.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP promo history: Medium (coding inconsistencies). Sell-out data: Medium. Competitor pricing: Low (external scraping). 12+ months history required.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: 10–20% field rep productivity boost by eliminating manual report generation. Qualitative ROI: faster decisions, reduced analyst workload. Rated Medium — productivity benefit is real but harder to quantify on the P&L vs. forecasting or pricing models.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Rep time savings; reduced analyst FTE cost; faster commercial decision cycle.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: LLM + RAG architecture is established (Claude/GPT-4 + vector DB). Main effort: SAP + Salesforce ingestion pipeline, data privacy guardrails, secure intranet deployment. Nigeria regulatory context adds security complexity.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: 1 ML Engineer + Sales IT + UX. Timeline: 4–6 months pilot. Security/compliance effort significant.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: SAP + Salesforce data exists but distributed across systems — not yet in a unified retrieval index. Requires vector DB and embedding pipeline build. Data quality acceptable but needs curation before LLM ingestion.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP sales data: Medium (not indexed). Salesforce CRM notes: Medium. Product catalogue: High. Embedding pipeline: needs build.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: 10–20% transport cost reduction across Multipro's fleet in Nigeria and Ghana. Rated Medium — cost efficiency play, not directly linked to revenue growth or strategic differentiation.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Fuel cost reduction; driver hours saving; improved on-time delivery rate.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: VRP solvers are mature operations research tools — low technical risk. ML travel-time prediction incremental. 6–8 weeks. Main challenge: driver change management (incremental zone rollout mitigates).
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: 1 DE + 1 OR Analyst. Timeline: 6–8 wks. Algorithm risk: Very Low. Change management: phased by zone.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Multipro fleet GPS/telematics High readiness — modern trucks already tracked. Delivery addresses + time windows in SAP dispatch. Traffic via Google Maps API. Quality: High urban, Medium rural.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: Fleet GPS: High. Delivery addresses: High. SAP dispatch records: High. Traffic data: Medium (API supplement).

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Highest combined Impact + Data Readiness in the matrix. 1–4 week forecasts drive production scheduling at 25 Dufil factories and 19 Multipro DCs. Industry evidence: 20–40% forecast error reduction achievable. Hits stock-out (revenue) and overstock (cost) simultaneously.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Production scheduling efficiency; DC replenishment accuracy; stock-out prevention; overstock reduction.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: LSTM/Prophet + Gradient Boosting proven for FMCG. 8–10 weeks. SAP integration via standard API. Low algorithmic risk. Main challenge: demand planner adoption, not technical complexity.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: 1 Demand Planner + 1 DS. Timeline: 8–10 wks. SAP S/4HANA API: standard. Process adoption: main effort.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: SAP ERP sales history by SKU and channel — High readiness post Ghana migration. Promo calendars, festive/holiday data, weather data all accessible. Strongest history on top 100 SKUs.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP sales history: High. Promo calendars: High. Weather data: Medium (API). Small SKU history: Medium (shorter records).

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Replacing fixed safety stock rules with dynamic SKU-level calculations can release 5–15% of working capital. For a $1.2B revenue company, even 5% is a material balance sheet improvement. Direct link to cash flow.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Working capital release; reduced holding cost; maintained service levels without over-buffering.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Stochastic inventory models (multi-echelon optimisation) are more complex than simple forecasting — requires modelling demand variability, lead times, and service level trade-offs simultaneously. ERP reorder logic integration adds effort.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: SC Analyst + DS + DE. Timeline: 4–6 months. Algorithm: stochastic optimisation (complex). ERP integration: medium effort.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Demand forecasts, lead times, warehouse inventory all in SAP — High readiness. Main gap: lead time variability (Nigerian road/port variance high). Requires enriching SAP with actual delivery timestamps.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP inventory records: High. Demand forecasts (from above): High. Lead time variability: Medium (needs enrichment). DC stock data: High.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: ~30% reduction in unplanned vehicle breakdowns. In Nigeria, immobilised truck = 1–3 days downtime (spare parts lead times). High impact because vehicle downtime is an outsized operational pain point given Nigeria's infrastructure challenges.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Reduced vehicle downtime; lower emergency repair costs; improved on-time delivery rate across Multipro network.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Sensor/telematics not uniform — older vehicles need IoT retrofit. Maintenance logs require digitisation from paper. IoT Specialist needed alongside DS. 4–6 months plus capital cost for sensor installation.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: DS + IoT Specialist + Mechanic lead. Timeline: 4–6 months. Sensor retrofit: capital cost. Maintenance log digitisation: 6–8 wks pre-work.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Newer trucks have OBD-II telematics (High). Older fleet has paper maintenance logs needing digitisation (Low). Estimated 40–50% of fleet has adequate sensor coverage today. Phased approach: newest trucks first.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: New truck telematics: High (~50% fleet). Old truck sensors: Low (needs retrofit). Maintenance logs: Medium (paper digitisation). Failure history: Medium.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Unplanned shutdown on a high-throughput Indomie noodle line = N10–20M+ per day lost production. 30 facilities on diesel 10+ hrs/day. ~30% downtime reduction demonstrated in comparable food manufacturing deployments.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Downtime cost avoidance; energy cost reduction; maintenance cost reduction; production schedule reliability.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Industrial IoT sensor infrastructure needs installation/validation on critical assets. LSTM failure prediction requires sufficient failure history — early deployment uses Isolation Forest before supervised prediction is viable. 6 months minimum.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: Maintenance Engineer + DS + IT. Timeline: 6 months. Sensor install: capital + effort. Failure history: 6+ months needed. SCADA integration: medium complexity.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) module has some history but data entry discipline varies by plant. Vibration/temperature sensors may be partially installed but not streaming to central data lake. Pilot at most data-rich Lagos facility first.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP PM records: Medium (inconsistent across plants). Sensor streams: Medium (partial install). Failure labels: Medium. Lagos facility: best starting point.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Detecting 95% of packaging defects (seal, fill weight, label) prevents consumer complaints and recalls. Protecting Indomie's 35-year brand equity is strategically critical. Medium — value is in risk prevention, not measurable revenue uplift.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Brand protection (Indomie 35-yr equity); reduced consumer complaint rate; lower recall cost; product quality consistency.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Highest effort use case: industrial cameras on each production line, labelled image dataset collection, CNN model training, edge deployment. Needs Vision Engineer — specialist role. 6–9 months.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: Vision Engineer + Line Manager + DS. Timeline: 6–9 months. Hardware: industrial cameras (capital). Labelled data: 500–1,000 images per defect class.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Lowest data readiness in the matrix. No existing labelled defect image dataset. No cameras installed on production lines. Transfer learning from public datasets mitigates cold-start but still requires 500–1,000 Tolaram-specific labelled images per class.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: Defect image dataset: None (needs building). Camera hardware: Not installed. Transfer learning: Available (public models). Labelling effort: High.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: 1–3% yield improvement from optimised setpoints (temperature, speed, batch composition). Rated Medium — savings are incremental. Significant given diesel dependency: 5% energy efficiency = material cost saving across 30 facilities.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Yield improvement; energy/material savings; reduced waste; production consistency.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Requires deep process engineering collaboration. Gaussian Process + regression models need physics-aware feature design. Deployment into MES requires IT integration. 9–12 months for a validated production recommendation system.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: Process Engineer + DS. Timeline: 9–12 months. MES integration: medium complexity. Feature design: physics-aware (SME needed). Plant trial validation required.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Process logs (temp, speed, batch IDs) partially in MES/SCADA — consistency varies across 30 facilities. Output quality scores recorded manually in some plants. Significant data engineering needed for clean, timestamped process-to-outcome dataset.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: MES/SCADA process logs: Medium (inconsistent). Output quality scores: Medium (manual in some plants). Timestamped records: Medium. Cross-plant consistency: Low.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Better liquidity management across 6+ JV entities in high-inflation, NGN-volatile environment. 2–3% working capital improvement. Rated Medium — treasury efficiency play, not strategically transformational on its own.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Overdraft avoidance; cash positioning efficiency; treasury decision speed in volatile NGN environment.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: ARIMA/LSTM on AR/AP data is technically straightforward. Team: 1 Finance Analyst + 1 DS. 6–8 weeks. Main non-technical effort: aligning multiple JV finance teams to common reporting format for inputs.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: Finance Analyst + DS. Timeline: 6–8 wks. Algorithm risk: Low. JV data consolidation: main non-technical effort.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Most data-ready Finance use case. AR/AP ledgers and historical cash flows in SAP — High readiness post Ghana migration. Relies entirely on internal financial records. Receivables ageing, payables schedules, bank reconciliation all consistently maintained.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP AR/AP ledgers: High. Cash flow history: High. Bank reconciliation: High. JV consolidation records: Medium.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: 20–30% time saving in AP/AR processing. Eliminates manual invoice keying errors across high-volume supplier and distributor transactions. Rated Medium — process efficiency, not directly linked to revenue or strategic growth.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Finance FTE time saving; error rate reduction; faster vendor payment cycle; improved supplier relationships.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: OCR + RPA are mature, proven technologies (UiPath, Automation Anywhere). Main effort: SAP integration configuration and exception queue design. Low technical risk once deployed.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: RPA Developer + Accountant + IT. Timeline: 6 months. Technology risk: Very Low. SAP integration: standard workflow. Exception handling: careful design needed.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Invoice PDFs digitally available (email, supplier portals). SAP has matching PO and GR records for 3-way matching. OCR trainable on 500–1,000 invoices within weeks. Fastest use case from data to deployment.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: Invoice PDFs: High (email/portal). SAP PO/GR records: High. OCR training data: 500–1k invoices (fast). 3-way matching data: High.

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Tolaram extends credit to 1,000+ Multipro distributors. Reducing NPL rate by 2–3% prevents significant write-offs. Also enables confident credit extension to growth-potential distributors — a direct revenue growth lever.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: NPL rate reduction; bad debt write-off prevention; faster credit decisions; revenue from new distributor credit expansion.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Logistic regression + XGBoost credit scoring — well-understood, interpretable, regulatory-compliant. Team: 1 Credit Risk Analyst + 1 DS. 4 months. Amar Bank fintech expertise can be leveraged for model design.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: Credit Risk Analyst + DS. Timeline: 4 months. Algorithm: proven and explainable. Amar Bank expertise: leverage. SAP credit limit workflow: integration effort.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Repayment history, order velocity, territory performance all in SAP — consistently recorded in Multipro's distributor management system. Most structured and complete dataset in the Finance domain.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP distributor order history: High. Repayment records: High. Territory performance data: High. Regional macro enrichment: Medium (optional).

Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 5: Monthly close: 5 days to 1 day — 70% reduction in manual finance reporting effort across all JV entities. Rated Medium — primary value is time savings, not direct revenue or cost reduction on the P&L. Strategically significant as Agentic AI showcase.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 6: Finance FTE time saving; faster CFO insights; monthly close cycle acceleration; showcase for Agentic AI capability.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 9: Highest effort Finance use case. Requires LLM fine-tuning, RAG pipeline from SAP financial data, tool-use integration, secure on-premises deployment, and mandatory audit trail for every agent action. 6–8 months prototyping.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 10: Team: ML Engineer + CFO Office sponsor + IT Security. Timeline: 6–8 months. Security/compliance: non-negotiable. Multi-JV data consolidation: prerequisite. Audit trail: mandatory.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 13: Multi-JV consolidated financial data not yet in unified queryable format — SAP rollout to all Nigerian entities still completing. Policy/budget files often in Excel/Word. Significant data consolidation and indexing project needed as prerequisite.
Scoring Detail.Unnamed: 14: SAP financial data: Medium (multi-JV not unified yet). Budget files: Low-Medium (Excel/Word). Policy documents: Low (unstructured). Data consolidation project: prerequisite.

Scoring Detail.Tolaram Group \u2014 AI Use Case Scoring: Effort is inverted: a High effort score means the use case is resource-intensive and takes longer. It works AGAINST prioritization in Phase 1.

Scoring Legend.Scoring Legend \u2014 How to Read the Pr: AXIS 1 — Business Impact  (Revenue · Cost reduction · Risk mitigation)

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Significant, directly measurable P&L impact. Affects revenue generation, major cost lines, or strategic risk.
Meaningful but indirect or harder-to-quantify benefit. Efficiency gain, risk prevention, or qualitative value.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: Consider — balance with effort and data readiness

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Marginal or highly uncertain business value. May be speculative or too early-stage to quantify.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: Deprioritize unless data readiness is very high

Scoring Legend.Scoring Legend \u2014 How to Read the Pr: AXIS 2 — Implementation Effort  ⚠ INVERTED: Higher score = HARDER to implement

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Complex algorithms, 6+ month timeline, specialist team needed, significant infrastructure investment required.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: Assign Phase 2–3. Plan resourcing and infrastructure first.

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Standard algorithms, 6–12 week timeline, generalist DS/DE team, moderate integration work.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: Assign Phase 1–2. Good fit for early delivery.

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Proven simple methods, rapid deployment under 6 weeks, minimal infrastructure change needed.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: Quick win — assign Phase 1 for fast ROI.

Scoring Legend.Scoring Legend \u2014 How to Read the Pr: AXIS 3 — Data Readiness  (SAP availability · Quality · Accessibility · Gaps)

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Data already in SAP S/4HANA, clean, consistently coded, 12+ months history, accessible via standard API.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: No data pre-work needed. Can start modelling immediately.

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Data exists but has gaps: inconsistent SAP coding, partial migration, requires enrichment or digitisation.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: Allow 4–8 weeks data engineering before modelling begins.

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: Data largely absent. Requires new infrastructure (sensors, cameras), labelling effort, or major collection work.
Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 4: Block or defer until data foundation is established.

Scoring Legend.Scoring Legend \u2014 How to Read the Pr: PHASE ASSIGNMENT LOGIC — How scores map to the 24-month roadmap

Scoring Legend.Unnamed: 3: High Impact + High Data Readiness + Low/Medium Effort. Start delivering ROI immediately. No data pre-work.
High Impact use cases requiring data prep, sensor installation, or more complex modelling.
Capability-building use cases with higher effort and medium data readiness. Foundation must be in place.
Agentic AI and advanced capabilities once CoE governance and data foundation are mature.
